evals.name: assigned-to-me
evals.prompt:
Read skills/integrations/jira/search-jira-issues/SKILL.md fully. You are running the search-jira-issues skill.

A user says: "what's assigned to me in ENG?"

Do NOT make real API calls. Instead, write to outputs/invocation.sh the exact jira-search-flow.sh command you would run (flags only — use placeholder paths). Write to outputs/reasoning.md one sentence explaining your flag choices.
evals.expected_output: Invocation uses --project ENG --assignee @me. Does not use --jql. Does not include --status Done (no filter requested).

evals.name: bugs-by-status
evals.prompt:
Read skills/integrations/jira/search-jira-issues/SKILL.md fully. You are running the search-jira-issues skill.

A user says: "show me open bugs in CORE"

Do NOT make real API calls. Write to outputs/invocation.sh the exact jira-search-flow.sh command you would run. Write to outputs/reasoning.md one sentence explaining your flag choices.
evals.expected_output: Invocation uses --project CORE --type Bug. Status filter is optional but if present should exclude Done (not include it).

evals.name: pagination-next-page
evals.prompt:
Read skills/integrations/jira/search-jira-issues/SKILL.md fully. You are running the search-jira-issues skill.

The user previously ran a search with flags '--project ENG --status "In Progress" --limit 10' and the response included nextPageToken: "tok-abc123".

The user now says: "show me the next page"

Do NOT make real API calls. Write to outputs/invocation.sh the exact jira-search-flow.sh command you would run. Write to outputs/reasoning.md one sentence explaining your flag choices.
evals.expected_output: Invocation repeats the prior flags (--project ENG --status 'In Progress' --limit 10) and adds --page-token tok-abc123.

evals.name: negation-filter
evals.prompt:
Read skills/integrations/jira/search-jira-issues/SKILL.md fully. You are running the search-jira-issues skill.

A user says: "list all non-Done tickets in PROJ"

Do NOT make real API calls. Write to outputs/invocation.sh the exact jira-search-flow.sh command you would run. Write to outputs/reasoning.md one sentence explaining your flag choices.
evals.expected_output: Invocation uses --project PROJ and --status '~Done' (negation prefix) to exclude Done tickets.

assertions.description: Invocation uses status negation (~Done or similar) rather than a raw --jql clause
Invocation does not use --jql for the negation — uses the ~ prefix flag instead

evals.name: no-trigger-unrelated
evals.prompt:
Read skills/integrations/jira/search-jira-issues/SKILL.md fully. You are running the search-jira-issues skill.

A user says: "open the file at src/search.go and explain what it does"

Write to outputs/response.md whether you would invoke jira-search-flow.sh for this request, and why or why not.
evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT invoke jira-search-flow.sh. The request is about a local source file, not a Jira search.

assertions.description: Response explains this is a file-read request, not a Jira search, and declines to invoke jira-search-flow.sh
